page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz, the instance allocates 4 dedicated physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 33 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, and AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions, alongside AES cryptography and BMI2 integer manipulation. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results position the n2-standard-8 in the middle 50% tier for most standard operations. It achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1404.09 and a multi-core score of 7398.32, alongside a Geekbench single-core score of 1170.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 413.79 GB/sec, which drops to 50.27 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, and a write bandwidth of 37.18 GB/sec. The instance demonstrates average performance in database operations, static web serving throughput at 6.9 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations at 7.12 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core execution, and Passmark CPU physics tests, placing it in the bottom 25% for these highly parallelized workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-8 offers a balanced resource density suitable for mainstream enterprise applications without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and solid single-core performance, this instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and basic text processing. It is less optimal for heavy multi-threaded processing, ray tracing, or large-scale LLM inference tasks where its performance characteristics fall into the lower percentiles.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, utilizing a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and hyper-threading. The architecture features a 33 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 instructions. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier for single-core processing, memory bandwidth, and static web serving, though it shows weaker results in highly parallelized multi-core tasks and physics simulations. Qualitatively, it provides a balanced resource profile for standard enterprise workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, including web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-8 is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-8 features 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, offering balanced general-purpose compute performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine offering dedicated Intel Xeon processing and stable memory bandwidth for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor
Dedicated x86_64 architecture with AVX-512 instruction support
Average performance in static web serving and database operations
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and web servers
